---
title: "Presentation title"
subtitle: "Subtitle or date"
author: "Your Name"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, quarto-theme-ki/ki.scss]
    slide-number: true
    footer: "Karolinska Institutet – ett medicinskt universitet"
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-color: "#4F0433"
---

<!-- =====================================================================
  KI Quarto reveal.js template.

  TO USE: copy this file to your PROJECT ROOT (next to the quarto-theme-ki/
  folder), rename it, and start editing. The theme path above
  (quarto-theme-ki/ki.scss) is relative to the project root.

  Slide types (see quarto-theme-ki/README.md):
    ## Heading                → white content slide
    ## Heading {.ljusbla}     → pale-blue section
    ## Heading {.ljusorange}  → pale-orange section
    ## Heading {.plum}        → dark-plum divider (white text)
    ## Heading {.blue}        → deep-blue divider (white text)
===================================================================== -->

## Content slide

Standard white slide with a plum heading and plum bullets:

- First point with some **bold emphasis**
- A [hyperlink](https://ki.se) in KI plum
- Body text in near-black DM Sans

## A pale-blue section {.ljusbla}

> "Table 1" typically includes a description of key demographic, social,
> and clinical characteristics of the study groups.
>
> — (Westreich & Greenland, 2013)

## A table

| Method            | Power | Notes              |
|-------------------|:-----:|--------------------|
| Unadjusted        | 0.62  | Baseline           |
| ANCOVA            | 0.78  | Adjusts covariates |
| Robust (sandwich) | 0.80  | Recommended        |

## Section divider {.plum}

Use `{.plum}` for a dark divider that echoes the title slide.

## Deep-blue divider {.blue}

Use `{.blue}` for a `#002C34` variant.

## A plot {.smaller}

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 3.5
# source("quarto-theme-ki/ki.R")   # theme_ki() + ki_color_palette
# library(ggplot2)
# ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, color = drv)) +
#   geom_point() +
#   scale_color_manual(aesthetics = c("fill", "color"), values = ki_color_palette) +
#   theme_ki()
```

Uncomment the chunk above (and remove `#| echo: false` to show the code).
